examples: There is a sense in which morality and expedience need not conflict.
Necessity has no law, and expedience is often one form of necessity.
Expedience: Balanced with all of this good advice, sometimes you just need to get it done.
Those ideals still light the world, and we will not give them up for expedience's sake.
It would be wonderful if we lived in a world were politicians embraced the most empirically compelling economic theory without regard for political expedience.
